name: Oysters Rockefeller
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 450 degrees.
2. Open the oysters, leaving them on the half shell and reserve the oyster liquid.
3. Pick over the spinach and remove any tough stems and blemished leaves. Rinse well and cook in a saucepan until wilted. Drain well. There should be about 2 cups.
4. Put the scallions, celery, and parsley into a food processor or electric blender and blend to about a cup.
5. Chop the garlic and anchovies together finely.
6. Heat 4 tablespoons butter in a skillet and add the scallion and celery mixture. Stir for about 1 minute and add the anchovy mixture. Cook, stirring, for another 1 minute.
7. Add the spinach to the skillet and stir to blend.
8. Heat the remaining 4 tablespoons butter in a saucepan and add the flour. Blend, stirring with a wire whisk, then add the oyster liquid and cream. Stir vigorously with the whisk. Season with Tabasco (no salt).
9. Add the spinach mixture and Pernod to the saucepan and stir until combined.
10. Spoon equal amounts of the mixture on top of each oyster and smooth over the tops. Sprinkle with Parmesan cheese.
11. Bake for about 25 minutes, or until piping hot.
